linting.notes: golangci-lint runs 25+ linters including: copyloopvar, dupl, errcheck, ginkgolinter, goconst, gocyclo, govet, ineffassign, lll, misspell, nakedret, prealloc, revive, staticcheck, unconvert, unparam, unused. Also includes formatters: gofmt and goimports.

testing.run_single_file: KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(./bin/setup-envtest use 1.31.0 --bin-dir ./bin -p path)" POD_NAMESPACE=default go test {file}
testing.run_single_test: KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(./bin/setup-envtest use 1.31.0 --bin-dir ./bin -p path)" POD_NAMESPACE=default go test {package} -ginkgo.focus '{test_name}'
testing.mock_patterns: Uses Ginkgo BeforeSuite/AfterSuite for test environment setup. envtest provides a real Kubernetes API server for controller tests.
testing.notes: Tests use envtest which requires downloading Kubernetes control plane binaries (etcd, kube-apiserver, kubectl) via setup-envtest. The test suite includes both unit tests and controller integration tests. E2E tests are excluded from 'make test' (located in test/e2e/).

gating_checks.name: Tests
gating_checks.command: go mod tidy && git diff --exit-code && make test
gating_checks.notes: Checks that go.mod is tidy before running tests

advisory_checks.name: E2E Tests
advisory_checks.notes: Requires Kind cluster, not run on every PR

ci.notes: All workflows trigger on both push and pull_request events. The test workflow verifies go.mod is tidy as part of the check. E2E tests require a Kubernetes cluster (Kind) and test the full controller deployment.

additional_targets.manifests: make manifests - generates CRDs, webhooks, RBAC

build.notes: Build requires controller-gen and kustomize tools (automatically downloaded via Makefile). The 'make build' target runs manifests, generate, fmt, and vet before building. Container build uses multi-stage Dockerfile with ubi9/go-toolset:1.25 as builder.

conventions.test_naming: Ginkgo BDD style: Describe/Context/It blocks. Test functions named TestXxx using Ginkgo RunSpecs
conventions.import_style: Standard library, third-party, internal packages separated by blank lines. Uses goimports for formatting.
conventions.code_organization: Kubernetes operator pattern: api/, internal/controller/, internal/webhook/, cmd/, config/
conventions.notes: Follows Kubebuilder project structure. Controller reconcilers in internal/controller/, webhooks in internal/webhook/. API types in api/. Uses controller-runtime for Kubernetes interactions.

test_commands.notes: All 17 test packages passed. Exit code 1 due to 'no such tool covdata' warnings (informational, not test failures). Coverage: ~6-18% per package.

container_recipe.test_single_file: GOTOOLCHAIN=auto KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS=$(./bin/setup-envtest use 1.31.0 --bin-dir ./bin -p path) POD_NAMESPACE=default go test {file}
container_recipe.test_single_test: GOTOOLCHAIN=auto KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS=$(./bin/setup-envtest use 1.31.0 --bin-dir ./bin -p path) POD_NAMESPACE=default go test {package} -ginkgo.focus '{test_name}'
container_recipe.notes: GOTOOLCHAIN=auto is required because the project uses Go 1.25.7, which is downloaded automatically by the Go toolchain. The base golang:1.23 image includes Go 1.23.12 by default, but GOTOOLCHAIN=auto allows it to download and use Go 1.25.7. envtest downloads real Kubernetes binaries (etcd, kube-apiserver) for controller testing.

results.notes: Built successfully, binary created at bin/manager
Linter found 0 issues - code is clean

results.notes: All 17 test packages passed. Exit code 1 is due to 'no such tool covdata' warnings (Go 1.25 feature not fully supported), but tests themselves succeeded. Coverage ranges from 0.2% to 18.5% per package.

validation.summary: install OK, build OK, lint OK (0 issues), tests OK (17/17 packages passed). Exit code 1 on tests is due to covdata tool warnings, not test failures.

gaps: E2E tests (test/e2e/) require a running Kubernetes cluster and are not included in 'make test'
The validate-manifests.sh script requires kustomize to be installed separately
Some generated code (manifests, CRDs) requires controller-gen tool
Coverage percentages are relatively low (0.2-18.5% per package), though this is common for controller code
Test exit code is non-zero due to 'covdata' tool warnings in Go 1.25, which may cause CI confusion
